raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I booked a hotel room at XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX for {$3400.00} for a future stay in XX/XX/XXXX. The booking was nonrefundable. I asked if I could call back after the election based on results. They said any consideration would be based on occupancy at the time. I am no longer going to XXXX XXXX in XX/XX/XXXX. 
I called the XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX to cancel my reservation - # XXXX. I then opened a dispute with XXXX XXXX to receive a refund - case # XXXX. They indicated I would receive an email in 3-5 business days with the resolution. I called to follow up on XX/XX/XXXX because I have not received a written resolution. The agent said the case was closed the same day I opened it by the hotel. I asked why and he said he did not know but would reopen the case. I then called American Express to assist me with the dispute with XXXX - case # XXXX. 
The XXXX website as of XX/XX/XXXX now indicates they are sold out on the days of my canceled reservation which means they resold my room. Accordingly, they are holding my funds as profit in addition to fees they will receive on the new reservation. They are double selling rooms. 
I am requesting your assistance to help me secure a refund and to investigate hotels in order to stop deceptive practices of not responding to requests for refunds, closing cases without proper documentation, and receiving double payment on hotel rooms. The credit card companies are stuck in the middle of this and other disputes when the mega businesses ( global hotels in my case ) will not work with consumers.
